Quote from: https://nusavifauna.wordpress.com/2012/07/31/ashy-tailorbird/

"..To differentiate the Ashy Tailorbird from the other tailorbirds in Singapore, note that the Ashy Tailorbird’s face is completely rust coloured, as opposed to the rust coloured crowns of the Common Tailorbird (Orthotomus sutorius) and the Dark-necked Tailorbird (Orthotomus atrogularis). In addition, the Ashy Tailorbird’s upper parts and tail are, as its name suggests, an ashy grey, in contrast to the olive-green of the Common Tailorbird and the Dark-necked Tailorbird. Other features of this tailorbird are its long slender bill, with the upper mandible curved at the tip, off-white abdomen and orange legs. It would seem, however, that the Ashy Tailorbird is less abundant than the Common Tailorbird on campus, though this isn’t always the case in other parts of Singapore.>>"

description: This adult Zebra Dove was seen to be foraging on the ground. It is small and slender with a long, narrow tail. It has brown upperparts with black and white barring. Its underparts are brown with black bars on the sides of the neck, breast and belly. Its face is blue-grey with bare blue skin around its eyes. Its sex is indeterminate.

Its plumage is almost entirely green. It has a red upperbill with a black tip. It has a black streak across the nape of its neck. This is an adult female, which can be differentiated from the male as it does not have a pink collar at its neck. This bird was seen resting on a tree.

This adult house crow is easily identified by its black glossy plumage, with greyish nape and breast. Its wings, tail and legs are black as well. It has a long bill with an arched beak. Its sex is indeterminate. This individual was vocalising with its partner.

This adult Yellow-vented Bulbul has a slight black crest, with a white face and black streak across its eye. Its upperparts are olive brown, and has a black bill and feet. Its sex is indeterminate. This individual is seen resting on some water plants with two other bulbuls.

This adult spotted dove was spotted to be resting in a tree with its partner. It has mostly light brown plumage, with streaks of dark of brown at its back and wings. Its head is grey, and the neck and underparts are grey-brown. It has a dark black beak, and red legs. Its most distinct feature is its spotted black collar at the base of its hind-neck. Its sex is indeterminate.

This adult male olive-backed sunbird showed yellow underparts with a dull brown back feathers. Its throat and breast is a dark, metallic blue-black, differentiating it from a female. It also has a thin black beak that has been adapted to suck nectar from flowers. This individual was observed to be resting on a tree near 2 zebra doves.

This adult Yellow Bittern was seen grooming itself. It has a pale-brown mantle, yellowish underparts, with light streaks down its foreneck and upper breast. This is a female as it has a reddish brown crown.

This adult Javan Myna is mainly grey, with a black head and wings. It also has white bases on its primary feathers. Similar to the Common Myna, it has yellow legs and bill. However, it does not have a yellow bare eye patch. Its sex is indeterminate. This myna was seen resting on a structure amidst a light drizzle.

This adult spotted dove has mostly light brown plumage, with streaks of dark of brown at its back and wings. Its head is grey, and the neck and underparts are grey-brown. It has a dark black beak, and red legs. Its most distinct feature is its spotted black collar at the base of its hind-neck. Its sex is indeterminate.

This adult Javan Myna is mainly grey, with a black head and wings. It also has white bases on its primary feathers. Similar to the Common Myna, it has yellow legs and bill. However, it does not have a yellow bare eye patch. Its sex is indeterminate.

This pair of adult Common Mynas have brown plumage, with black wings and heads. They have yellow bill and legs. They also have yellow bare eye  skins. They have a white patch underneath their wings, which is visible during flight. This pair is seen taking shelter during a drizzle. Their sex is indeterminate.

An adult individual standing on a lamppost. It has a turquoise crown, back and remiges, a clean white collar and belly. It also has white lore and a black eye-stripe. It has a black bill with a flesh-coloured base for the lower mandible. Was unable to determine sex.
